name: Bean Soup
recipeIngredient: 1 cup red kidney beans, dried, rinsed and drained
1 cup white lima beans, dried, rinsed and drained
1 cup chick-peas (garbanzo beans), dried, rinsed and drained
2 cups stewed tomatoes, low sodium (14 1/2 ounces)
4 cups condensed chicken broth (3-10 1/2 ounces cans)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, rinse and drain all dried beans. Cover with 8 cups of fresh water and refrigerate. Soak for a minimum of 4 hours, preferably overnight.
2. In an 8-quart pot, heat olive oil over medium-high heat. Cook onion and garlic for 2-3 minutes, stirring frequently, until the onion is translucent.
3. Drain beans and rinse under cold running water. Add beans to the pot with tomatoes, broth, and water. Bring to a boil, stirring occasionally.
4. Reduce heat to medium-low and cook soup uncovered for 1 hour and 50 minutes, or until beans are tender. Stir occasionally.
5. Stir in ham cubes, lemon juice, and pepper. Cook for 10-15 minutes until ham is heated through. Garnish with parsley before serving.
Serving Size: 1 cup
Yield: 12 servings
